Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Understand the Goal The user wants me to construct a Python dictionary representing a Log Skeleton for a given order fulfillment process scenario The Log Skeleton contains specific declarative constraints equivalence always before always after never together activ freq and directly follows

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Analyze the Constraints and Format equivalence Set of tuples A B meaning if A occurs it has the same number of occurrences as B always before Set of tuples A B meaning if A occurs B must have occurred previously always after Set of tuples A B meaning if A occurs B must occur in a following event never together Set of tuples A B meaning A and B cannot co exist in the same case activ freq Dict mapping activity to a set of allowed occurrence counts directly follows Set of tuples A B meaning if A occurs B must occur immediately after All keys must be exactly as specified equivalence always before always after never together activ freq directly follows

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Analyze the Scenario and Activities Activities RO CA RI PI QC PO GSL D IC RP Process flow typical order fulfillment RO to CA to RI to PI to QC to PO to GSL to D IC and RP are financial invoicing steps Usually IC happens after packing or dispatch and RP happens after IC

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Lets map out a plausible flow RO starts the case and occurs exactly once CA must happen after RO and occurs exactly once RI must happen after CA and occurs exactly once PI must happen after RI and occurs exactly once QC must happen after PI and occurs exactly once PO must happen after QC and occurs exactly once GSL must happen after PO and occurs exactly once D must happen after GSL and occurs exactly once IC happens after D and occurs exactly once RP happens after IC and occurs exactly once

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: I need to express these relationships using the declarative constraints provided I should pick a representative set of constraints that makes sense for the scenario not necessarily every possible pair The prompt does not specify exact constraints so I have to infer reasonable ones based on standard business process logic

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: Equivalence Activities that always occur together with the same frequency RO and D might be equivalent if we assume every received order is eventually dispatched IC and RP might also be equivalent I will use RO D and IC RP as plausible pairs

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Always before A B means if A occurs B must have occurred previously Examples CA RO RI CA PI RI QC PI PO QC GSL PO D GSL IC D RP IC

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Always after A B means if A occurs B must occur later Examples RO CA CA RI RI PI PI QC QC PO PO GSL GSL D D IC IC RP

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Never together In this standard flow the prompt does not mention cancellations or alternative flows I will leave it as an empty set

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Activity occurrences In a standard order fulfillment each activity occurs exactly once per order so RO CA RI PI QC PO GSL D IC RP all get frequency set 1

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Directly follows A B means B immediately follows A Examples RO CA CA RI RI PI PI QC QC PO PO GSL GSL D D IC IC RP

Name: Validation - PE
Text: I need to be careful with the definitions Always Before A B means B before A Always After A B means A before B Directly Follows A B means A immediately before B This matches standard declarative process mining

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Lets verify the constraint definitions again to ensure I am using them correctly Equivalence A B same occurrences Always Before A B B before A Always After A B A before B Never Together A B cannot coexist Activity Occurrences A set 1 Directly Follows A B A immediately before B

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - IND
Text: Wait is there redundancy always after and directly follows overlap That is fine declarative models often have overlapping constraints

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Lets reconsider activ freq The prompt example uses zero one and one two I will assume all occur exactly once and stick to set 1 for all to keep it clean

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Lets reconsider never together I could add a hypothetical constraint but the scenario is linear and no mutually exclusive branch is described I will just use an empty set

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Lets verify the tuple order for always before If the first activity occurs then the second activity should have been executed previously so CA RO means if CA occurs RO must have occurred previously For always after RO CA means if RO occurs CA must occur later For directly follows RO CA means if RO occurs CA occurs immediately after

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: I will construct the dictionary with keys equivalence always before always after never together activ freq directly follows and values matching the required types using the inferred sequential constraints and exact once frequencies

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Final check of the prompt The Log Skeleton is expressed as a Python dictionary containing the keys equivalence always before always after never together activ freq directly follows Values are sets of tuples for constraints and a dictionary for activity frequencies All good
